message:
feat: add ACP (Agent Client Protocol) support for Zed editor integration

Implement full ACP stdio server via `pi --acp`, enabling pi_agent_rust
to run as an external agent in Zed editor and other ACP-compatible hosts.

Protocol methods:
- initialize, session/new, session/list, session/load, session/resume
- prompt (with streaming progress notifications)
- cancel, read_text_file, write_text_file, shutdown/exit

The ACP layer is a thin JSON-RPC 2.0 adapter over pi's existing session,
prompt, and tool infrastructure. Streaming maps AgentEvent variants to
prompt/progress notifications with content items (textDelta, toolUse,
toolResult, etc.).

Closes #43

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -49,6 +49,7 @@ fn known_long_option(name: &str) -> Option<LongOptionSpec> {
         | "no-session"
         | "no-migrations"
         | "print"
+        | "acp"
         | "verbose"
         | "no-tools"
         | "no-extensions"
@@ -355,6 +356,11 @@ pub struct Cli {
     #[arg(short = 'p', long)]
     pub print: bool,
 
+    /// Start in ACP (Agent Client Protocol) mode for Zed editor integration.
+    /// Reads JSON-RPC 2.0 requests from stdin and writes responses to stdout.
+    #[arg(long)]
+    pub acp: bool,
+
     /// Force verbose startup
     #[arg(long)]
     pub verbose: bool,
